newSummary: Gain bonuses on Pilot checks checks when near allies.

name: Frightful Presence
oldBenefit: As a standard action, you may make a Persuasion check to intimidate all enemies within 6 squares. On a success, targets move -1 step down the condition track.
newEffect: As a standard action, you may make a Persuasion check to intimidate all enemies within 6 squares. On a success, targets move -1 step down the condition track.
oldSummary: As a standard action, you may make a Persuasion check to intimidate all enemies within 6 squares.
newSummary: As a standard action, you may make a Persuasion check to intimidate all enemies within 6 squares.

name: Instinctive Defense
oldBenefit: Spend a Force Point to increase all Defenses by +2 for 1 round.
newEffect: On your turn, as a Free Action, you can spend a Force Point to increase all of your Defenses by 2 until the start of your next turn.
oldSummary: Spend a Force Point to increase all Defenses by +2 for 1 round.
newSummary: Spend a Force Point to increase all Defenses by +2 for 1 round.

name: Cybernetic Surgery
oldBenefit: Install a Cybernetic Prosthesis onto a living being.
newEffect: You can install Cybernetic Devices on living beings. The surgical procedure takes 1 hour of uninterrupted work, after which you must make a DC 20 Treat Injury check. If the check succeeds, the Cybernetic Device is installed correctly. If the check fails, the device is not properly installed; however, you can try again after another uninterrupted hour of surgery.
oldSummary: Install a Cybernetic Prosthesis onto a living being.
newSummary: Install a Cybernetic Prosthesis onto a living being.

name: Suppression Fire
oldPrerequisite: Strength 13, Burst Fire, Weapon Proficiency (Heavy Weapons)
newPrerequisite: Strength 13, Burst Fire, Weapon Proficiency (Heavy Weapons)
oldBenefit: You can lay down a hail of blaster fire to pin your opponents.
newEffect: When you use the Aid Another action to impose a penalty to an enemy's attack rolls and your attack roll exceeds the target's Will Defense, that enemy must end its next turn in a position where it has Cover from you if possible. Targets whose level is equal to or higher than your Character Level are immune to the effect of Suppression Fire. This is a Mind-Affecting Fear effect.
oldSummary: You can lay down a hail of blaster fire to pin your opponents.
newSummary: You can lay down a hail of blaster fire to pin your opponents.

name: A Few Maneuvers
oldBenefit: You can weave, juke, and roll to avoid enemy fire in the thick of combat.
newEffect: When piloting a Vehicle of Colossal size or smaller, you grant a +2 dodge bonus to your Vehicle's Reflex Defense. Furthermore, if you are the target of a missile or torpedo attack and the attack roll misses by 5 or more, the projectile self-destructs harmlessly.
oldSummary: You can weave, juke, and roll to avoid enemy fire in the thick of combat.
newSummary: You can weave, juke, and roll to avoid enemy fire in the thick of combat.

name: Rebel Military Training
oldBenefit: Gain a dodge bonus to Reflex Defense when using Running Attack.
newEffect: Whenever you use the Running Attack feat to move both before and after making an attack, you gain a +2 dodge bonus to your Reflex Defense until the start of your next turn.
oldSummary: Gain a dodge bonus to Reflex Defense when using Running Attack.
newSummary: Gain a dodge bonus to Reflex Defense when using Running Attack.

name: Master of Disguise
oldBenefit: Gain +5 to creating a Deceptive Appearance or a Forged Document, and reduce the penalty for rushing.
newEffect: You gain a +5 insight bonus to your Deception check when creating a Deceptive Appearance. In addition, you can rush the process by taking a -2 penalty to the Deception check.
oldSummary: Gain +5 to creating a Deceptive Appearance or a Forged Document, and reduce the penalty for rushing.
newSummary: Gain +5 to creating a Deceptive Appearance or a Forged Document, and reduce the penalty for rushing.

name: Attack Combo (Ranged)
oldBenefit: Deal +1 die of damage on ranged attacks until the end of your next turn after hitting an enemy with two ranged attacks.
newEffect: If you hit a single target with two consecutive ranged attacks during the same turn, any additional ranged attacks you make until the end of your next turn (including Attacks of Opportunity and attacks made as Reactions) deal +1 die of damage on a hit. This extra damage stacks with extra damage granted by other Feats or Talents.
oldSummary: Deal +1 die of damage on ranged attacks until the end of your next turn after hitting an enemy with two ranged attacks.
newSummary: Deal +1 die of damage on ranged attacks until the end of your next turn after hitting an enemy with two ranged attacks.

name: Far Shot
oldBenefit: Range penalties for Short-, Medium-, and Long-ranged attacks are reduced.
newEffect: When you use a ranged weapon against targets at Short, Medium, or Long Range, the Range category is considered one less. In other words, you take no penalty on ranged attack rolls made at Short Range, a -2 penalty on ranged attack rolls made against targets at Medium Range, and a -5 penalty on ranged attack rolls made against targets at Long Range.
oldSummary: Range penalties for Short-, Medium-, and Long-ranged attacks are reduced.
newSummary: Range penalties for Short-, Medium-, and Long-ranged attacks are reduced.

name: Long Haft Strike
oldBenefit: You can wield a long-handled weapon as a Double Weapon.
newEffect: When you use a Lightsaber Pike or a Long-Handle Lightsaber, you can attack with both ends of the weapon, treating it as a double weapon.
oldSummary: You can wield a long-handled weapon as a Double Weapon.
newSummary: You can wield a long-handled weapon as a Double Weapon.

name: Tireless Squad
oldBenefit: Gain bonuses on Endurance checks when near allies.
newEffect: Gain bonuses on Endurance checks when near allies.
oldSummary: Gain bonuses on Endurance checks when near allies.
newSummary: Gain bonuses on Endurance checks when near allies.

name: Improvised Weapon Mastery
oldBenefit: Treat Improvised Weapons as Simple Weapons, deal +1d6 points of damage.
newEffect: You treat all Improvised Weapons as Simple Weapons, and you can use them without penalty and with Talents and Feats designed for use with Simple Weapons. You also deal an additional 1d6 points of damage on a successful hit.
oldSummary: Treat Improvised Weapons as Simple Weapons, deal +1d6 points of damage.
newSummary: Treat Improvised Weapons as Simple Weapons, deal +1d6 points of damage.

name: Hew
oldBenefit: When you use Power Attack, you ignore an amount of damage reduction equal to your base attack bonus.
newEffect: When you use Power Attack, you ignore an amount of damage reduction equal to your base attack bonus.
oldSummary: When you use Power Attack, you ignore an amount of damage reduction equal to your base attack bonus.
newSummary: When you use Power Attack, you ignore an amount of damage reduction equal to your base attack bonus.

name: Mission Specialist
oldBenefit: Grant bonus to allies' Untrained Skill Checks with a particular Skill.
newEffect: Choose one Skill in which you are Trained. Allies who are not Trained in that Skill gain a +2 competence bonus to the Skill while they are within 12 squares of you.
oldSummary: Grant bonus to allies' Untrained Skill Checks with a particular Skill.
newSummary: Grant bonus to allies' Untrained Skill Checks with a particular Skill.

name: Mechanical Martial Arts
oldPrerequisite: Droid, Martial Arts I, Base Attack Bonus +1
newPrerequisite: Droid, Martial Arts I, Base Attack Bonus +1
oldBenefit: Give an enemy a -5 penalty on attack and damage rolls for one round after striking it in Unarmed combat.
newEffect: When you successfully damage an enemy with an Unarmed Attack, the enemy takes a -5 penalty to all melee attack rolls and damage rolls until the start of your next turn.
oldSummary: Give an enemy a -5 penalty on attack and damage rolls for one round after striking it in Unarmed combat.
newSummary: Give an enemy a -5 penalty on attack and damage rolls for one round after striking it in Unarmed combat.

name: Force of Personality
oldBenefit: Use Wisdom or Charisma to determine Will Defense.
newEffect: You can use either your Wisdom modifier or your Charisma modifier to determine your Will Defense.
oldSummary: Use Wisdom or Charisma to determine Will Defense.
newSummary: Use Wisdom or Charisma to determine Will Defense.

name: Mon Calamari Shipwright
oldBenefit: Reroute Power more quickly and without the risk of failure.
newEffect: Reroute Power more quickly and without the risk of failure.
oldSummary: Reroute Power more quickly and without the risk of failure.
newSummary: Reroute Power more quickly and without the risk of failure.

name: Improved Opportunistic Trickery
oldBenefit: Sacrifice Attack of Opportunity to reduce target's Reflex Defense by 5.
newEffect: Whenever an opponent provokes an Attack of Opportunity from you, once per turn you can sacrifice the attack to reduce the target's Reflex Defense by 5 until the end of the target's next turn.
oldSummary: Sacrifice Attack of Opportunity to reduce target's Reflex Defense by 5.
newSummary: Sacrifice Attack of Opportunity to reduce target's Reflex Defense by 5.

name: Unleashed
newPrerequisite: Have a Destiny, Receive the Gamemaster's Approval
oldBenefit: Unlocks Unleashed Abilities.
newEffect: You can activate Unleashed Abilities by spending a Destiny Point. You must also have the Force Sensitivity Feat in order to activate Force Unleashed Abilities for Force Powers and Force Talents.
oldSummary: Unlocks Unleashed Abilities.
newSummary: Unlocks Unleashed Abilities.

name: Regenerative Healing
oldBenefit: Regain more Hit Points (but more slowly) when you catch a Second Wind.
newEffect: Regain more Hit Points (but more slowly) when you catch a Second Wind.
oldSummary: Regain more Hit Points (but more slowly) when you catch a Second Wind.
newSummary: Regain more Hit Points (but more slowly) when you catch a Second Wind.

name: Increased Agility
oldBenefit: Increase Climb Speed, Swim Speed, and Jump distance by 2 squares.
newEffect: You increase your Climb Speed, Swim Speed and Jump distance by +2 squares and do not lose your Dexterity bonus to Reflex Defense when climbing.
oldSummary: Increase Climb Speed, Swim Speed, and Jump distance by 2 squares.
newSummary: Increase Climb Speed, Swim Speed, and Jump distance by 2 squares.

name: Lightning Reflexes
oldBenefit: You gain a +2 bonus to your Reflex Defense.
newEffect: You gain a +2 bonus to your Reflex Defense.
oldSummary: You gain a +2 bonus to your Reflex Defense.
newSummary: You gain a +2 bonus to your Reflex Defense.

name: Biotech Specialist
oldBenefit: Make custom modifications to Yuuzhan Vong Biotech.
newEffect:
You can modify a Biotech device, suit of armor, weapon, or Vehicle so that it gains a specific trait. You can perform only one modification at a time. Unless noted otherwise, you cannot grant more than one benefit to a single device, suit of armor, weapon, or Vehicle, and you cannot apply the same benefit more than once.

Before beginning the modification, you must pay one tenth the cost of the device, suit of armor, weapon, or Vehicle you wish to modify or 1,000 credits, whichever is more. Completing the modification requires one day per 1,000 credits of the modification cost. At the end of this time, make a DC 20 Mechanics check; you can't Take 10 or Take 20 on this check. If the check succeeds, the modification is completed successfully, and the object gains the desired trait. If the check fails, you lose all credits spent making the modification, and the object doesn't gain the desired trait. However, you may start over if you wish.

Due to the specialized nature of Biotechnology, only other characters with the Biotech Specialist Feat can assist you, reducing proportionately the time needed to complete the modification. At the end of the modification process, they can make a Mechanics check to aid your check.

The market value of a modified item is equal to the base cost of the item + double the cost of the modifications made to it (not including credits wasted on failed attempts).

ARMOR TRAITS | BENEFIT
Agile Armor | The armor's maximum Dexterity bonus increases by 1.
Fortifying Armor | The armor's Equipment bonus to Fortitude Defense increases by 1.
Protective Armor | The armor's Armor bonus to Reflex Defense increases by 1.
DEVICE TRAITS | BENEFIT
Add Device to Creature | Alter a bioengineered creature to accept a biotech or standard technological enhancement such as a miniaturized Sensor Pack.
Enhanced Strength | Increase the device's Strength score by 2.
Improved Durability | The device's Damage Reduction increases by 1, and it gains extra Hit Points equal to one-quarter of its maximum Hit Points.
Mastercraft Device | Skill Checks made using the device gain a +1 Equipment bonus, or the device's existing Equipment bonus increases by 1.
VEHICLE TRAITS | BENEFIT
Dovin Basal Enhancement | Increase the effects of a Dovin Basal to grant one of the following benefits to a Starship: +1 additional square of movement at Starship Scale, +2 SR, or +1 die of damage when using a Dovin Basal.
Enhanced Dexterity | Increase the Vehicle's Dexterity score by 2.
Improved Speed | Increase the Vehicle's Speed by one-quarter of its base Speed (minimum 1 square).
WEAPON TRAITS | BENEFIT
Improved Accuracy | The weapon gains a +1 Equipment bonus on attack rolls.
Improved Damage | The weapon deals +2 points of damage with a successful hit. If the weapon has a damage multiplier (for example, 6d10x2), apply the extra damage before applying the multiplier.
Selective Fire | An Autofire -only ranged weapon can be set to fire single shots, or a single-shot weapon can be made to have an Autofire mode. This cannot be applied to weapons that have a Burst radius or Splash radius.
oldSummary: Make custom modifications to Yuuzhan Vong Biotech.
newSummary: Make custom modifications to Yuuzhan Vong Biotech.

name: Instinctive Perception
oldBenefit: Gain a temporary Force Point when your Perception reroll is lower.
newEffect: Gain a temporary Force Point when your Perception reroll is lower.
oldSummary: Gain a temporary Force Point when your Perception reroll is lower.
newSummary: Gain a temporary Force Point when your Perception reroll is lower.

name: Extra Rage
oldBenefit: Rage one additional time per day.
newEffect: You can Rage one additional time per day.
oldSummary: Rage one additional time per day.
newSummary: Rage one additional time per day.

name: Angled Throw
oldBenefit: Ignore Cover with Grenades and grenadelike weapons.
newEffect: When throwing a Grenade or grenadelike weapon, you can attempt to bounce it off a wall or other surface close to your target. If you attack roll exceeds a Reflex Defense of 15, you ignore Cover and Improved Cover (but not Total Cover) with your attack.
oldSummary: Ignore Cover with Grenades and grenadelike weapons.
newSummary: Ignore Cover with Grenades and grenadelike weapons.

name: Knock Heads
oldBenefit: You may knock two opponents' heads together after a successful Multi-Grab.
newEffect: After successfully using the Multi-Grab Feat against two targets that are both adjacent to you and each other, you can immediately knock their heads together, dealing automatic bludgeoning damage to each creature equal to 1d6 plus your Strength modifier. When comparing the damage to each creature's Damage Threshold, treat their Damage Thresholds as if they were 5 points lower. Both creatures are still considered Grabbed at the end of using this Feat.
oldSummary: You may knock two opponents' heads together after a successful Multi-Grab.
newSummary: You may knock two opponents' heads together after a successful Multi-Grab.

name: Controlled Rage
oldBenefit: Enter Rage as a Free Action, and end Rage at will.
newEffect: You enter a Rage as a Free Action. Your Rage ends 1 round after you declare it is finished.
oldSummary: Enter Rage as a Free Action, and end Rage at will.
newSummary: Enter Rage as a Free Action, and end Rage at will.

name: Precise Shot
oldBenefit: No -5 penalty for shooting into melee.
newEffect: You can shoot or throw a ranged weapon at an opponent engaged in melee combat with one or more of your allies without taking the standard -5 penalty.
oldSummary: No -5 penalty for shooting into melee.
newSummary: No -5 penalty for shooting into melee.

name: Pin
oldBenefit: Grappled opponent is Pinned for one round, can't move, and and loses its Dexterity bonus to Reflex Defense.
newEffect: If you succeed on a Grappling attack and your opponent fails the opposed Grapple check, your opponent is automatically Pinned until the start of your next turn. A Pinned creature can't move or take any Actions while Pinned, and it loses its Dexterity bonus (if any) to Reflex Defense.
oldSummary: Grappled opponent is Pinned for one round, can't move, and and loses its Dexterity bonus to Reflex Defense.
newSummary: Grappled opponent is Pinned for one round, can't move, and and loses its Dexterity bonus to Reflex Defense.

name: Forceful Blast
oldPrerequisite: Weapon Proficiency (Simple Weapons), Base Attack Bonus +1
newPrerequisite: Weapon Proficiency (Simple Weapons), Base Attack Bonus +1
oldBenefit: Move a target damaged by your Grenade attack.
newEffect:
When you damage a Large or smaller creature with a Grenade or Thermal Detonator, compare the result of your attack roll to the target's Fortitude Defense. If your result equals or exceeds the target's Fortitude Defense, you can move the target 1 square in any direction as a Free Action.

If the Grenade deals damage to multiple eligible creatures, you can use this benefit against all of them. You can't move a target that's being Grabbed or Grappled, and you can't move a target into a solid object or into another creature's Fighting Space.
oldSummary: Move a target damaged by your Grenade attack.
newSummary: Move a target damaged by your Grenade attack.

name: Focused Rage
oldBenefit: When Raging, use Skills that require patience at a -5 penalty.
newEffect: While Raging, you can use Skills that require patience and concentration, at a -5 penalty.
oldSummary: When Raging, use Skills that require patience at a -5 penalty.
newSummary: When Raging, use Skills that require patience at a -5 penalty.

name: Point Blank Shot
oldBenefit: +1 bonus on ranged attacks and damage against foes within Point-Blank Range.
newEffect: You get a +1 bonus on attack and damage rolls with ranged weapons against opponents within Point-Blank Range.
oldSummary: +1 bonus on ranged attacks and damage against foes within Point-Blank Range.
newSummary: +1 bonus on ranged attacks and damage against foes within Point-Blank Range.

name: Weapon Focus
oldBenefit: +1 bonus on attack rolls with chosen weapon.
newEffect: Choose a single Exotic Weapon or weapon group, you gain a +1 bonus on all attack rolls you make using the selected Exotic Weapon or weapon group.
oldSummary: +1 bonus on attack rolls with chosen weapon.
newSummary: +1 bonus on attack rolls with chosen weapon.

name: Coordinated Barrage
oldBenefit: Allow an ally to deal more damage when you aid their attack.
newEffect: When you Aid Another ally's attack, for every 3 points that the ally's attack roll beats the target's Reflex Defense, the attack deals +1 die of damage. The maximum number of bonus damage dice the attack receives is equal to the number of allies with this Feat that aided on the attack roll or +5 dice, whichever is lower.
oldSummary: Allow an ally to deal more damage when you aid their attack.
newSummary: Allow an ally to deal more damage when you aid their attack.

name: Recall
newPrerequisite: Trained in at least one Knowledge Skill
oldBenefit: Reroll Knowledge skill check, keeping the better result.
newEffect: Once per day, you can reroll any check for a Knowledge Skill that you are Trained in, using the better result.
oldSummary: Reroll Knowledge skill check, keeping the better result.
newSummary: Reroll Knowledge skill check, keeping the better result.

name: Dual Weapon Mastery II
oldPrerequisite: Dexterity 15, Base Attack Bonus +6, Dual Weapon Mastery I
newPrerequisite: Dexterity 15, Dual Weapon Mastery I, Base Attack Bonus +6
oldBenefit: Take a -2 penalty on attacks when attacking with two weapons, or both ends of a double weapon.
newEffect: When you attack with two weapons, or with both ends of a double weapon as part of a Full Attack Action, you take a -2 penalty (Instead of a -10 penalty) on all attack rolls until the start of your next turn. You only gain this reduced penalty if you are wielding a weapon with which you are proficient.
oldSummary: Take a -2 penalty on attacks when attacking with two weapons, or both ends of a double weapon.
newSummary: Take a -2 penalty on attacks when attacking with two weapons, or both ends of a double weapon.

name: Knock Prone
oldBenefit: You may attempt to knock an opponent prone with a melee attack. If successful, the target falls prone.
newEffect: You may attempt to knock an opponent prone with a melee attack. If successful, the target falls prone.
oldSummary: You may attempt to knock an opponent prone with a melee attack.
newSummary: You may attempt to knock an opponent prone with a melee attack.

name: Autofire Assault
oldBenefit: Decrease penalty on Autofire attack and inflict extra damage when sustaining fire in the same squares in consecutive rounds.
newEffect: When wielding a weapon for which you possess the Weapon Focus Feat, and you target the same area with an Autofire attack that you made an Autofire attack against on your last turn, the penalty on the Autofire attack is reduced to -2 (-1 if using a Braced Autofire -only weapon or the Controlled Burst Talent) and you deal +1 die of damage on a hit.
oldSummary: Decrease penalty on Autofire attack and inflict extra damage when sustaining fire in the same squares in consecutive rounds.
newSummary: Decrease penalty on Autofire attack and inflict extra damage when sustaining fire in the same squares in consecutive rounds.

name: Withdrawal Strike
oldBenefit: Adjacent opponents can not use the Withdraw Action.
newEffect: When taking this Feat, select a single Exotic Weapon (Melee) or melee weapon group with which you are proficient. When wielding that chosen Exotic Weapon (Melee) or melee weapon from the chosen group, adjacent opponents may not Withdraw from squares you threaten. They may still make Acrobatics checks to Tumble as normal.
oldSummary: Adjacent opponents can not use the Withdraw Action.
newSummary: Adjacent opponents can not use the Withdraw Action.

name: Hyperblazer
oldBenefit: Cut all calculation time and Use Computer penalties by half when making Use Computer checks for Astrogation in the Hyperspace tangle. Reduce Hyperspace mapping time by half.
newEffect: When traversing the Hyperspace Tangle, you reduce by half the calculation time and penalties on Use Computer checks to Astrogate (See Hyperspace Navigation Challenges). You also reduce Hyperspace Mapping time by half when mapping new Hyperspace Routes.
oldSummary: Cut all calculation time and Use Computer penalties by half when making Use Computer checks for Astrogation in the Hyperspace tangle.
newSummary: Cut all calculation time and Use Computer penalties by half when making Use Computer checks for Astrogation in the Hyperspace tangle. Reduce Hyperspace mapping time by half.

name: Improved Rapid Strike
oldBenefit: Trade -5 penalty on attack roll for +2 dice of damage when using a Light Melee Weapon.
newEffect: When using a Light Melee Weapon or Lightsaber with the Rapid Strike Feat, you can take a -5 penalty to your attack roll to gain +2 dice of damage on a successful strike.
oldSummary: Trade -5 penalty on attack roll for +2 dice of damage when using a Light Melee Weapon.
newSummary: Trade -5 penalty on attack roll for +2 dice of damage when using a Light Melee Weapon.

name: Burst of Speed
oldBenefit: Move Speed twice as Move Action.
newEffect: As a Move Action, you can Move up to twice your speed. At the end of your movement, you move -1 step along the Condition Track.
oldSummary: Move Speed twice as Move Action.
newSummary: Move Speed twice as Move Action.

name: Rapid Strike
oldBenefit: Take a -2 penalty on a melee attack roll to deal +1 die of damage.
newEffect: When using a melee weapon with which you are proficient, you may make two strikes as a single attack against a single target. You take a -2 penalty on your attack roll, but deal +1 die of damage with a successful attack.
oldSummary: Take a -2 penalty on a melee attack roll to deal +1 die of damage.
newSummary: Take a -2 penalty on a melee attack roll to deal +1 die of damage.

name: Hobbling Strike
oldPrerequisite: Sneak Attack or Rapid Shot or Rapid Strike
newPrerequisite: Sneak Attack or Rapid Shot or Rapid Strike.
oldBenefit: Reduce the target's Speed instead of dealing damage.
newEffect: Whenever you would deal extra damage by using Sneak Attack, Rapid Shot, or Rapid Strike, you can reduce the target's Speed by 1 square until the end of the encounter, instead of dealing the extra damage.
oldSummary: Reduce the target's Speed instead of dealing damage.
newSummary: Reduce the target's Speed instead of dealing damage.

name: Acrobatic Dodge
oldBenefit: When a melee attack misses you, move into an adjacent square without provoking an Attack of Opportunity.
newEffect: Once per encounter, as a Reaction, if a melee attack misses you, you can immediately move into an adjacent square without provoking an Attack of Opportunity.
oldSummary: When a melee attack misses you, move into an adjacent square without provoking an Attack of Opportunity.
newSummary: When a melee attack misses you, move into an adjacent square without provoking an Attack of Opportunity.

name: Implant Training
oldBenefit: You don't move additional steps down the Condition Track due to your Implants.
newEffect: You are not moved an extra step down the Condition Track when you would normally be moved down the Condition Track.
oldSummary: You don't move additional steps down the Condition Track due to your Implants.
newSummary: You don't move additional steps down the Condition Track due to your Implants.

name: Improved Disarm
oldBenefit: Gain +5 bonus on melee attacks to Disarm an opponent.
newEffect: You gain a +5 bonus on any melee attack roll made to Disarm an opponent. In addition, if you fail to Disarm an opponent, he doesn't get to make a free attack against you.
oldSummary: Gain +5 bonus on melee attacks to Disarm an opponent.
newSummary: Gain +5 bonus on melee attacks to Disarm an opponent.

name: Harm’s Way
oldBenefit: As a reaction, you may take damage dealt to an adjacent ally instead of them.
newEffect: As a reaction, you may take damage dealt to an adjacent ally instead of them.
oldSummary: As a reaction, you may take damage dealt to an adjacent ally instead of them.
newSummary: As a reaction, you may take damage dealt to an adjacent ally instead of them.

name: Multi-Targeting
oldBenefit: You can spread your Aim action across multiple consecutive rounds.
newEffect: You can use Swift Actions across more than one round to Aim at a target with a weapon with which you are proficient. You can attack different targets prior to the completion of your Aim Action. If the subject of your Aim is out of your line of sight for any reason, you lose your Aim and must start again.
oldSummary: You can spread your Aim action across multiple consecutive rounds.
newSummary: You can spread your Aim action across multiple consecutive rounds.

name: Momentum Strike
oldBenefit: You know how to put the weight of your momentum into attacks while riding a Mount or Speeder Bike.
newEffect: When riding a Beast as a Mount or on a Speeder Bike (as a passenger or a Pilot), you add +1 die of damage to any melee attacks if your Mount or Vehicle has already moved at least its speed this turn.
oldSummary: You know how to put the weight of your momentum into attacks while riding a Mount or Speeder Bike.
newSummary: You know how to put the weight of your momentum into attacks while riding a Mount or Speeder Bike.

name: Advanced Melee Weapon Proficiency
oldBenefit: You are proficient with advanced melee weapons and do not take the -5 penalty when using them.
newEffect: You are proficient with advanced melee weapons and do not take the -5 penalty when using them.
oldSummary: You are proficient with advanced melee weapons and do not take the -5 penalty when using them.
newSummary: You are proficient with advanced melee weapons and do not take the -5 penalty when using them.

name: Master Tracker
oldBenefit: Increase the die type when adding Force Points to a Survival check.
newEffect: Increase the die type when adding Force Points to a Survival check.
oldSummary: Increase the die type when adding Force Points to a Survival check.
newSummary: Increase the die type when adding Force Points to a Survival check.

name: Hunter's Instincts
oldBenefit: Reroll Perception checks, always keeping the better result.
newEffect: Reroll Perception checks, always keeping the better result.
oldSummary: Reroll Perception checks, always keeping the better result.
newSummary: Reroll Perception checks, always keeping the better result.

name: Jedi Heritage
oldBenefit: Gain extra Force Powers when you take the Force Training Feat.
newEffect: Gain extra Force Powers when you take the Force Training Feat.
oldSummary: Gain extra Force Powers when you take the Force Training Feat.
newSummary: Gain extra Force Powers when you take the Force Training Feat.

name: Bothan Will
oldBenefit: Gain a bonus to Will Defense when an enemy fails to overcome your iron will.
newEffect: Gain a bonus to Will Defense when an enemy fails to overcome your iron will.
oldSummary: Gain a bonus to Will Defense when an enemy fails to overcome your iron will.
newSummary: Gain a bonus to Will Defense when an enemy fails to overcome your iron will.

name: Natural Leader
oldBenefit: You become the leader of an Organization.
newEffect: You become the leader of an Organization of your design. The Organization has a scale equal to one-half your Heroic Level plus your Charisma bonus and continues to grow in scale as you gain levels. You automatically begin with a +10 bonus to your Organization Score for your new Organization.
oldSummary: You become the leader of an Organization.
newSummary: You become the leader of an Organization.

name: Armor Proficiency (medium)
oldBenefit: No penalty on attacks and no Armor Check Penalty while wearing Light Armor or Medium Armor.
newEffect: When you wear Medium Armor, you take no Armor Check Penalty on attack rolls or Skill Checks. Additionally, you benefit from all of the armor's special Equipment bonuses (if any).
oldSummary: No penalty on attacks and no Armor Check Penalty while wearing Light Armor or Medium Armor.
newSummary: No penalty on attacks and no Armor Check Penalty while wearing Light Armor or Medium Armor.

name: Logic Upgrade: Skill Swap
oldBenefit: Swap a Trained Skill for an Untrained Skill on the fly.
newEffect: Swap a Trained Skill for an Untrained Skill on the fly.
oldSummary: Swap a Trained Skill for an Untrained Skill on the fly.
newSummary: Swap a Trained Skill for an Untrained Skill on the fly.

name: Droid Shield Mastery
oldBenefit: Automatically Recharge Shields in two Swift Actions.
newEffect: You automatically succeed on Endurance checks to restore your Shield Rating by 5 points (up to your normal Shield Rating). Additionally, you replenish your shields in two Swift Actions.
oldSummary: Automatically Recharge Shields in two Swift Actions.
newSummary: Automatically Recharge Shields in two Swift Actions.

name: Frightening Presence
oldBenefit: When you use Frightful Presence, targets that fail their Will Defense check move -2 steps down the condition track instead of -1.
newEffect: When you use Frightful Presence, targets that fail their Will Defense check move -2 steps down the condition track instead of -1.
oldSummary: When you use Frightful Presence, targets that fail their Will Defense check move -2 steps down the condition track instead of -1.
newSummary: When you use Frightful Presence, targets that fail their Will Defense check move -2 steps down the condition track instead of -1.

name: Wary Defender
oldBenefit: Gain a +2 bonus to Fortitude Defense and Will Defense while Fighting Defensively.
newEffect: When you use the Fight Defensively Action, you gain a +2 competence bonus to your Fortitude Defense and Will Defense until the beginning of your next turn.
oldSummary: Gain a +2 bonus to Fortitude Defense and Will Defense while Fighting Defensively.
newSummary: Gain a +2 bonus to Fortitude Defense and Will Defense while Fighting Defensively.

name: Vehicle Systems Expertise
oldBenefit: Recharge Shields or Reroute Power on a Vehicle faster than normal.
newEffect: You may use the Recharge Shields or Reroute Power Actions by spending two Swift Actions. Also, once per encounter, you may attempt to use either of these Actions as a single Swift Action by making a DC 30 Mechanics check.
oldSummary: Recharge Shields or Reroute Power on a Vehicle faster than normal.
newSummary: Recharge Shields or Reroute Power on a Vehicle faster than normal.

name: Indomitable Personality
oldBenefit: Add your Charisma bonus to your Will Defense as a Reaction.
newEffect: Once per encounter, you can add your Charisma modifier to your Will Defense as a Reaction; this benefit lasts until the end of your next turn.
oldSummary: Add your Charisma bonus to your Will Defense as a Reaction.
newSummary: Add your Charisma bonus to your Will Defense as a Reaction.

name: Fast Swimmer
oldBenefit: Gain a bonus to your Swim Speed.
newEffect: Gain a bonus to your Swim Speed.
oldSummary: Gain a bonus to your Swim Speed.
newSummary: Gain a bonus to your Swim Speed.

name: Officer Candidacy Training
oldBenefit: Gain a bonus to your Rank and Privilege Score.
newEffect: You gain a +2 bonus to your Rank and Privilege Organization Score.
oldSummary: Gain a bonus to your Rank and Privilege Score.
newSummary: Gain a bonus to your Rank and Privilege Score.

name: Critical Strike
oldPrerequisite: Base Attack Bonus +9, Weapon Focus (Chosen Weapon)
newPrerequisite: Base Attack Bonus +9, Weapon Focus (Chosen Weapon)
oldBenefit: Increase critical threat range of an attack.
newEffect:
While wielding a weapon for which you possess the Weapon Focus feat, you can take two consecutive Swift Actions in the same round to increase the critical threat range of your next melee attack by 1.

Thus, if a weapon scores a Critical Hit on a Natural 20, it can score a Critical Hit on a natural roll of 19 or 20 instead. However, any roll other than a Natural 20 is not considered an automatic hit; if you roll a Natural 19 and miss the target, you do not score a Critical Hit. You lose the benefits of Critical Strike if you lose line of sight to your target or if you take any other Action before making your attack.
oldSummary: Increase critical threat range of an attack.
newSummary: Increase critical threat range of an attack.

name: Pistoleer
oldBenefit: Gain benefits when using certain Pistols.
newEffect:
When you make a ranged attack with a Pistol with which you are proficient, you gain one of the following benefits, depending on the kind of Pistol you use.

- Blaster Pistol: You treat the Blaster Pistol as an Accurate Weapon.
- Heavy Blaster Pistol: You do not treat the Heavy Blaster Pistol as an Inaccurate Weapon.
- Hold-Out Blaster Pistol: If you fire a Hold-Out Blaster Pistol at a target that has not yet acted in combat, you gain a +2 bonus to your attack rolls with that weapon against that target. This bonus applies to all such attacks until the target attacks.
oldSummary: Gain benefits when using certain Pistols.
newSummary: Gain benefits when using certain Pistols.

name: Tactical Genius
oldPrerequisite: Starship Tactics, Vehicular Combat, Trained in Pilot
newPrerequisite: Starship Tactics, Vehicular Combat, Trained in Pilot
oldBenefit: You are a master at using multiple starship tactics over the course of long space combats.
newEffect: You regain all spent Starship Maneuvers at the end of any round in which you roll a Natural 20 on an attack roll.
oldSummary: You are a master at using multiple starship tactics over the course of long space combats.
newSummary: You are a master at using multiple starship tactics over the course of long space combats.

name: Wilderness Specialists
oldBenefit: Gain bonuses on Survival checks when near allies.
newEffect: Gain bonuses on Survival checks when near allies.
oldSummary: Gain bonuses on Survival checks when near allies.
newSummary: Gain bonuses on Survival checks when near allies.

name: Skill Challenge: Last Resort
oldBenefit: Reroll a third failed Skill Check during a Skill Challenge, keeping the better result.
newEffect: Reroll a third failed Skill Check during a Skill Challenge, keeping the better result.
oldSummary: Reroll a third failed Skill Check during a Skill Challenge, keeping the better result.
newSummary: Reroll a third failed Skill Check during a Skill Challenge, keeping the better result.

name: Forceful Weapon
oldPrerequisite: Trained in Use the Force, Battle Strike
newPrerequisite: Trained in Use the Force, Battle Strike
oldBenefit: You gain a +2 bonus on Use the Force checks made to activate Battle Strike.
newEffect: You gain a +2 bonus on Use the Force checks made to activate Battle Strike.
oldSummary: You gain a +2 bonus on Use the Force checks made to activate Battle Strike.
newSummary: You gain a +2 bonus on Use the Force checks made to activate Battle Strike.

name: Bowcaster Marksman
oldBenefit: Gain a bonus to damage rolls when you spend a Force Point on a Bowcaster attack.
newEffect: Gain a bonus to damage rolls when you spend a Force Point on a Bowcaster attack.
oldSummary: Gain a bonus to damage rolls when you spend a Force Point on a Bowcaster attack.
newSummary: Gain a bonus to damage rolls when you spend a Force Point on a Bowcaster attack.

name: Forceful Warrior
oldBenefit: You gain a +2 bonus on melee attack rolls made while using a Force power.
newEffect: You gain a +2 bonus on melee attack rolls made while using a Force power.
oldSummary: You gain a +2 bonus on melee attack rolls made while using a Force power.
newSummary: You gain a +2 bonus on melee attack rolls made while using a Force power.

name: Mighty Swing
oldBenefit: Spend two Swift Actions to deal extra damage in melee.
newEffect: You can spend two Swift Actions in the same round to deal +1 die of damage on your next melee attack in the same round.
oldSummary: Spend two Swift Actions to deal extra damage in melee.
newSummary: Spend two Swift Actions to deal extra damage in melee.

name: Spacer's Surge
oldBenefit: Gain a temporary Force Point when you roll a Natural 20 on a Pilot check.
newEffect: Gain a temporary Force Point when you roll a Natural 20 on a Pilot check.
oldSummary: Gain a temporary Force Point when you roll a Natural 20 on a Pilot check.
newSummary: Gain a temporary Force Point when you roll a Natural 20 on a Pilot check.

name: Force Sensitivity
oldBenefit: You can make Use the Force checks and gain access to Force Talents.
newEffect: You can make Use the Force checks, and Use the Force is considered a Class Skill for you. In addition, whenever you gain a Talent, you have the option of selecting a Force Talent instead. You must meet the prerequisites of the Force Talent to select it.
oldSummary: You can make Use the Force checks and gain access to Force Talents.
newSummary: You can make Use the Force checks and gain access to Force Talents.

name: Wookiee Grip
oldBenefit: Wield two-handed weapons in a single hand.
newEffect: When using a weapon in which you are proficient and that normally requires two hands, you can wield that weapon with a single hand. You take a -2 penalty to attack rolls with the weapon when it is used in this manner.
oldSummary: Wield two-handed weapons in a single hand.
newSummary: Wield two-handed weapons in a single hand.

name: Prime Shot
oldBenefit: Gain a bonus to attack rolls when closer to your target than your allies.
newEffect: If none of your allies are closer to your target than you are when you make a ranged attack, you gain a +1 circumstance bonus to the attack roll. Your target must be at Short Range or closer to gain this bonus.
oldSummary: Gain a bonus to attack rolls when closer to your target than your allies.
newSummary: Gain a bonus to attack rolls when closer to your target than your allies.

name: Shrewd Bargainer
oldBenefit: Suppress opponents' insight and morale bonuses to Will Defense.
newEffect: Suppress opponents' insight and morale bonuses to Will Defense.
oldSummary: Suppress opponents' insight and morale bonuses to Will Defense.
newSummary: Suppress opponents' insight and morale bonuses to Will Defense.

name: Brilliant Defense
oldBenefit: Add your Intelligence bonus to your Reflex Defense as a Reaction.
newEffect: Once per encounter, you can add your Intelligence modifier to your Reflex Defense as a Reaction; this benefit lasts until the start of your next turn.
oldSummary: Add your Intelligence bonus to your Reflex Defense as a Reaction.
newSummary: Add your Intelligence bonus to your Reflex Defense as a Reaction.

name: Friends in Low Places
oldBenefit: Reduce Black Market cost multiplier for Restricted Items by 1.
newEffect: Whenever you would acquire a license for a Restricted or Military object, you can substitute a Gather Information check for a Knowledge (Bureaucracy) check. Reduce the Black Market cost multiplier of such items by 1.
oldSummary: Reduce Black Market cost multiplier for Restricted Items by 1.
newSummary: Reduce Black Market cost multiplier for Restricted Items by 1.

name: Grab Back
oldBenefit: As a Reaction, make a Grab or Grapple attack following a failed Grab or Grapple attack made against you.
newEffect: You gain a +2 bonus to your Reflex Defense against Grab and Grapple attacks. If an enemy misses you when making a Grab or Grapple attack, you can immediately make your own Grab attack against the same enemy as a Reaction.
oldSummary: As a Reaction, make a Grab or Grapple attack following a failed Grab or Grapple attack made against you.
newSummary: As a Reaction, make a Grab or Grapple attack following a failed Grab or Grapple attack made against you.

name: Shield Surge
oldPrerequisite: Droid or Cyborg Hybrid, Trained in Mechanics
newPrerequisite: Droid or Cyborg Hybrid, Trained in Mechanics
oldBenefit: Trade Vehicle's Shield Rating for damage taken.
newEffect: As a Reaction, when your Vehicle takes damage above its Shield Rating, after the Shield Rating is reduced you can reduce the amount of damage sustained by the Vehicle by an amount up to the remaining Shield Rating. However, the Shield Rating is immediately reduced by one point for every point of damage reduced.
oldSummary: Trade Vehicle's Shield Rating for damage taken.
newSummary: Trade Vehicle's Shield Rating for damage taken.

name: Deep Sight
oldBenefit: Gain Darkvision and ignore Total Concealment from darkness.
newEffect: Gain Darkvision and ignore Total Concealment from darkness.
oldSummary: Gain Darkvision and ignore Total Concealment from darkness.
newSummary: Gain Darkvision and ignore Total Concealment from darkness.

name: Trample
oldBenefit: When using the Mounted Charge Action, attack enemies between you and your target.
newEffect: When you use the Mounted Charge Action, your Mount can make a single melee attack as a Free Action. While Charging, compare the result to the Reflex Defense of each enemy whose square you Charge through. Each enemy hit by the attack takes Claw, Gore, or Slam damage from the Mount's Natural Weapons plus the Mount's Strength modifier. You or your Mount still can make a regular attack at the end of the Charge.
oldSummary: When using the Mounted Charge Action, attack enemies between you and your target.
newSummary: When using the Mounted Charge Action, attack enemies between you and your target.

name: Resilient Reflexes
oldBenefit: You gain a +2 bonus on Reflex Defense against effects that target Dexterity and may reroll a failed check once per encounter.
newEffect: You gain a +2 bonus on Reflex Defense against effects that target Dexterity and may reroll a failed check once per encounter.
oldSummary: You gain a +2 bonus on Reflex Defense against effects that target Dexterity and may reroll a failed check once per encounter.
newSummary: You gain a +2 bonus on Reflex Defense against effects that target Dexterity and may reroll a failed check once per encounter.

name: Weapon Proficiency (Pistols)
oldBenefit: You are proficient with pistols and do not take the -5 penalty when using them.
newEffect: You are proficient with pistols and do not take the -5 penalty when using them.
oldSummary: You are proficient with pistols and do not take the -5 penalty when using them.
newSummary: You are proficient with pistols and do not take the -5 penalty when using them.

name: Powerful Charge
oldPrerequisite: Medium or larger sized, Base Attack Bonus +1
newPrerequisite: Medium or larger size, Base Attack Bonus +1
oldBenefit: Gain +2 bonus on your attack roll while Charging, and deal extra damage.
newEffect: When you Charge, you gain an additional +2 bonus on your melee attack roll. If your melee attack hits, you deal additional damage equal to one-half your level.
oldSummary: Gain +2 bonus on your attack roll while Charging, and deal extra damage.
newSummary: Gain +2 bonus on your attack roll while Charging, and deal extra damage.

name: Meat Shield
oldPrerequisite: Point-Blank Shot, Precise Shot, Base Attack Bonus +4
newPrerequisite: Point-Blank Shot, Precise Shot, Base Attack Bonus +4
oldBenefit: Gain Cover bonus equal to opponent's Cover bonus.
newEffect: Whenever an opponent attacks you while in Cover provided by another character, creature, or Droid, you are treated as being in Cover from attacks by that opponent.
oldSummary: Gain Cover bonus equal to opponent's Cover bonus.
newSummary: Gain Cover bonus equal to opponent's Cover bonus.

name: Fleet-Footed
oldBenefit: +2 Speed when making a Running Attack.
newEffect: If you Move both before and after you make an attack using the Running Attack Feat, your Speed is increased by 2 squares until the end of your turn.
oldSummary: +2 Speed when making a Running Attack.
newSummary: +2 Speed when making a Running Attack.

name: Cornered
oldBenefit: When unable to Withdraw, +2 on attacks.
newEffect: Whenever you are Threatened by an opponent and unable to take the Withdraw Action, you gain a +2 bonus on attack rolls against opponents that threaten you.

newSummary: When unable to Withdraw, +2 on attacks.

name: Starship Designer
oldBenefit: You are trained to design (and redesign) Starships.
newEffect:
You can design a Starship from scratch. Doing so takes a minimum of 30 days and a DC 25 Mechanics check, adding 1 to the DC and 1 to the time for every additional 100,000 credits of the design's final cost beyond the first 100,000 credits (even a genius Starship Designer can spend years working out the blueprints for a Star Destroyer). Other characters Trained in the Mechanics skill may use the Aid Another action to assist your Mechanics check; if any assisting character also has the Starship Designer Feat, divide the Starship's cost by the number of characters who have this Feat (including the primary designer) when determining the time required.

When making Starship Modifications, you never treat the work as though it were a Nonstandard Modification, even if it normally would be. Additionally, you can make custom modifications to Starships. Each of these modifications has a base cost of 5,000 credits (multiplied by the Starship Size Cost Modifier) and requires a DC 25 Mechanics check. On a failed check, the modification fails; you must spend half again as much time and money to make a second attempt, and you gain a +2 bonus on your Mechanics check for each attempt after the first.

Installation of a custom modification normally takes one day per 5,000 credits of the final cost. The same system can be customized multiple times, but doing so becomes increasingly difficult and expensive. For every previous custom modification on a given system, increase the DC by 5 and the base cost by 10,000 credits. No system can be customized more than three times. Multiple benefits from successive customizations stack. Typical customizations are described below. The Gamemaster may allow other modifications.

- Add Emplacement: You add 1 unused Emplacement Point to a Starship.
- Improve Hull: You add a number of Hit Points to a Starship equal to 1/2 its Strength score (rounded down to the nearest multiple of 10).
- Improve Hyperdrive: You can reengineer a hyperdrive to improve its class by 1 step. The hyperdrive obeys all the restrictions of its new class.
- Improve Shields: The Shield Rating of the shields is increased by 5.
- Improve Weapons: You grant one Vehicle Weapon a +1 Equipment bonus on attack rolls.
oldSummary: You are trained to design (and redesign) Starships.
newSummary: You are trained to design (and redesign) Starships.

name: Sensor Link
oldBenefit: Share sensor data instantly with an ally.
newEffect:
As a Swift Action, you can broadcast the input from your audio, visual, and special sensors to a Droid ally, Comlink, communications system, or holographic receiver within 24 squares.

That ally is aware of anything you are aware of, and can use the Aid Another Action to aid on your Perception checks even if you do not have line of sight to one another. If both you and your ally have Sensor Link, you share information simultaneously, granting a +2 bonus to Perception checks.
oldSummary: Share sensor data instantly with an ally.
newSummary: Share sensor data instantly with an ally.

newSummary: Gain a +2 Species bonus to your Fortitude Defense.

Choose one Weapon Group. You are proficient with all weapons of the selected group. Weapon Groups include the following:

- Simple Weapons (includes Simple Weapons (Melee), Simple Weapons (Ranged), and Grenades)
- Pistols

newEffect:
You can use your Perception modifier instead of your Gather Information modifier when making Gather Information checks. You are considered Trained in the Gather Information skill. If you are entitled to a Gather Information check reroll, you can reroll your Perception check instead (subject to the same circumstances and limitations).

Additionally, when you have favorable conditions for your Gather Information check (such as from operating on your home planet), you reduce by half the time it takes to make a Gather Information check.

newSummary: Gather Information with Perception skill, and do it faster.

name: Echani Training
oldBenefit:
You can double the damage bonus provided by your Strength modifier to Unarmed Attacks (minimum +1) provided you make only one Unarmed attack this turn.

Once per encounter when you deal damage to a target with an Unarmed Attack you may make an immediate Unarmed Attack against the target's Fortitude Defense as a Free Action; if successful, you knock your opponent to the ground (Prone) in their current square, provided they are no more than one size larger than you.

Special: Targets gain a bonus to their Fortitude Defense against being knocked Prone based on their size: Large +5, Huge +10, Gargantuan +20, Colossal +50. Unusually stable creatures (such as those with four legs) gain a +5 bonus to their Fortitude Defense against being knock Prone as well.
newEffect:
You can double the damage bonus provided by your Strength modifier to Unarmed Attacks (minimum +1) provided you make only one Unarmed attack this turn.

Once per encounter when you deal damage to a target with an Unarmed Attack you may make an immediate Unarmed Attack against the target's Fortitude Defense as a Free Action; if successful, you knock your opponent to the ground (Prone) in their current square, provided they are no more than one size larger than you.
oldSummary: You can double the damage bonus provided by your Strength modifier to Unarmed Attacks (minimum +1) provided you make only one Unarmed attack this turn.
newSummary: In Unarmed combat, increase damage, and knock opponent Prone on Critical Hit.

newSummary: Move when you catch a Second Wind.

newSummary: Attack multiple targets as you move past them.

newSummary: You may choose to exclude a number of squares equal to your Dexterity modifier from the area of effect of your autofire attacks.

name: Metamorph
oldPrerequisite: Constitution 13, Shapeshift Species Trait, Trained in Deception
newPrerequisite: Constitution 13, Shapeshift Species Trait, Trained in Deception
oldBenefit: Change your size.
newEffect:
As a Full-Round Action, you can change your mass when you use your Shapeshift Species Trait, increasing or decreasing your size by one step. You can maintain this form for a number of rounds per day equal to your Constitution score.

If you reduce your size to Small, you gain a +1 size bonus to your Reflex Defense and a +5 size bonus on Stealth checks, but your Carrying Capacity is three-quarters of what it was prior to your use of Metamorph.

If you increase your size to Large, you take a -1 penalty to your Reflex Defense and a -5 penalty on Stealth checks. However, your Carrying Capacity doubles, you gain a +5 size bonus to your Damage Threshold, and your Reach increases by 1 square.
oldSummary: Change your size.
newSummary: Change your size.
